_patch_meta.description: MTI status assignments for 16 unreconciled terms, Phase 2 flags from Session B analysis, and registry status update.

operations.description: Set MTI status and reconciled flag for G5590G (psuchē: soul) — primary Greek anchor

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. Primary Greek psuchē anchor. 825 occurrences, 46 verses in export (volume-sampling noted in PH2-182-002). Analytically central.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G5590H (psuchē: life)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. Secondary Greek anchor. NT parallel to H5315H. 42 occurrences, 33 verses. Includes the key laying-down-of-life sayings (Joh 10-15; Mk 8:35; 1Jo 3:16).

operations.description: Set MTI status for G5590I (psuchē: myself)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. Reflexive psuchē. 6 verses. All analytically significant: Magnificat (Luk 1:46), Gethsemane (Mk 14:34), Heb 10:38, 3Jo 2.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G5590J (psuchē: person)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. NT parallel to H5315J. 8 verses. Includes Acts Pentecost counting (Act 2:41), Eze 18:4 parallel (Rom 13:1), 1Pe 3:20.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G5590K (psuchē: animal)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 occurrence, 0 verses in export (NO_VERSES flag). NT parallel to H5315K. Confirmed in corpus (Rev 8:9; 16:3). Thin data — insufficient for independent analysis.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G5591 (psuchikos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. Analytically critical: the only NT adjective marking soul as a mode of existence contrasted with spirit. 5 verses (1Cor 2:14; 15:44, 46; Jam 3:15; Jude 19). Phase 2 flag PH2-182-003 raised for spirit-soul boundary study.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G1634 (ekpsuchō)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 3 verses (Act 5:5, 10; 12:23). All three are divine judgment contexts. Preserves the breath/expire root (ekpsucho = breathe out/expire). Analytically useful for the soul-and-death dimension.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G3642 (oligopsuchos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (1Th 5:14). Small-souled / fainthearted. Placed in community pastoral triad (idle, fainthearted, weak). Analytically informative as a soul-level capacity descriptor.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G1374 (dipsuchos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 2 verses (Jam 1:8; 4:8). Double-minded / two-souled. Analytically significant: encodes inner division at the soul level. Opposite of sumpsuchos / integrity.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G4861 (sumpsuchos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (Phil 2:2). One-souled / harmonious. Part of a relational-coherence compound cluster with isopsuchos (Phil 2:20).

operations.description: Set MTI status for G2473 (isopsuchos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (Phil 2:20). Equal-souled / like-minded. Part of relational-coherence compound cluster with sumpsuchos.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G2174 (eupsucheō)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (Phil 2:19). Good-souled / be of good cheer. Minor compound; lexically informative. The lifted soul as a NT pastoral category.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G0674 (apopsuchō)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (Luk 21:26). Fainting with fear / breathing out. Preserves breath-root in Greek parallel to H5314 na.phash. Thin data but verse is analytically useful in eschatological soul-fear context.

operations.description: Set MTI status for G0895 (apsuchos)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 1 verse (1Cor 14:7). Lifeless / soulless. Used by Paul to argue by contrast: instruments without a soul cannot produce meaningful speech. Thin data but verse is analytically useful.

operations.description: Set MTI status for H5314 (na.phash)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 confirmation. 3 verses (Exo 23:12; 31:17; 2Sa 16:14). Verb: be refreshed / breathe again. Etymological anchor for the breath-root underlying ne.phesh. Exo 31:17 applies it to God — analytically significant.

operations.description: Set MTI status for H5317 (no.phet, honey)

set.status_note: Session B v3.0 boundary decision. H5317 is not a soul term. Entered registry via STEP relatedNos for H5315G. Five verses (Psa 19:10; Pro 5:3; Pro 24:13; Pro 27:7; Song 4:11) illuminate the appetite dimension (H5315L) through the honey-as-archetype-of-desire metaphor. Retained as Phase 2 cross-reference enrichment only. See PH2-182-001.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: H5317 honey — appetite dimension enrichment

record.description: H5317 (no.phet, honey) is not a soul term but enriches the appetite dimension of ne.phesh (H5315L). Key verse: Pro 27:7 (one who is full loathes honey, but to one who is hungry everything bitter is sweet). Cross-reference with Pro 16:24 (gracious words are sweetness to the soul). Independent Phase 2 study of the honey/appetite metaphor cluster recommended before appetite-dimension synthesis.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: G5590G verse coverage limitation

record.description: G5590G (psuchē: soul) has 825 occurrences but only 46 verses in the export (ratio 0.056). Synthesis-level conclusions about the NT use of psuchē must be held provisional. Priority Phase 2 coverage: Synoptic soul-sayings in full; Pauline pastoral soul-vocabulary; 1 Peter (concentrated pastoral soul theology in 1Pe 1-4); Revelation soul-references.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: psuchikos and the spirit-soul boundary

record.description: G5591 (psuchikos) marks soul-level existence as contrasted with spirit-level (1Cor 2:14; 15:44-46; Jam 3:15; Jude 19). Full mapping of the soul-spirit boundary requires cross-registry analysis with spirit (H7307/G4151) and heart registries. Flag for Session D comparative analysis: soul vs spirit as levels of the inner person.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: Heb 4:12 soul-spirit division

record.description: Heb 4:12 claims the word of God pierces to the division of soul and spirit. This implies soul and spirit are normally indistinguishable, becoming separable only under divine precision. Requires dedicated Phase 2 exegetical study of the soul-spirit boundary in Hebrews, in context of the book's anthropology. Cannot be resolved by single-word analysis.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: Isa 53 servant soul and atonement

record.description: Isa 53:10-12 contains three ne.phesh (H5315H) uses constituting the OT's most concentrated soul-and-atonement text (soul as guilt offering; anguish of soul; soul poured out to death). Requires Phase 2 study in full Isaianic context with NT ransom sayings (Mat 20:28; Mk 10:45; Joh 10:11-17; 1Jo 3:16). Critical for the soul-substitution-atonement nexus in Session D.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: dead soul and post-mortem identity

record.description: H5315M (ne.phesh: dead body) applies ne.phesh paradoxically to a corpse, suggesting the term carries the form or identity of the person even after life departs. Intersects with Sheol-vocabulary and Psalms' hope for soul-rescue from the pit (Psa 16:10; 49:15). Phase 2 research required: the soul in OT eschatology and the semantics of dead ne.phesh in relation to post-mortem identity.

operations.description: Update registry record to reflect Session B completion

set.notes:
4 terms (4 extracted). 1 part. Session A Instruction v7. MTI: extracted.
[AUDIT 2026-03-23] result=REVIEW  terms=24  verses=757  run_id=RUN-20260323_202200-AUDIT_WORD
[SESSION B 2026-03-24] Session B v3.0 complete. Analysis document: WA-182-soul-analysis-2026-03-24.md. 16 MTI terms reconciled. 6 Phase 2 flags raised (PH2-182-001 through PH2-182-006). H5317 classified as phase2_enrichment. Session D priority: Wave 1 structural anchor — soul as the constitutive dimension of Circle Two.

_patch_summary.h5317_decision: phase2_enrichment — not a primary soul term; retained as appetite-dimension cross-reference only
